name: Alaskan Sourdough Fruitcake
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 cup Golden, seedless, and puffed raisins, combined
1 cup Blackberry Cordial or other wine
3 cups Candied fruit, green and red (cherries, pineapple, orange, citron, and ginger)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak raisins and currants in 1 cup of wine overnight.
2. Remove starter from refrigerator and set, tightly covered, in a warm place overnight. It should be in at least a two-cup container as it will just about double its volume overnight.
3. In the morning, cream butter with sugar and beat in eggs and lemon rind.
4. Drain wine from raisins into the creamed mixture.
5. Stir in starter and 3 cups of flour sifted with baking soda, salt, and spices.
6. Sprinkle the remaining 1 cup of flour over the fruit and nuts in a large bowl.
7. Toss and shake until well-coated.
8. Add to batter and mix thoroughly.
9. Turn into loaf pans which have been generously buttered.
10. Let stand in warm place for 30 minutes.
11. Bake in oven preheated to 300°F with a pan of water on the floor of the oven and rack as near as possible in the middle of the oven. Bake about 2-1/2 hours for medium-sized loaves, watching carefully to ensure they do not brown too quickly.
12. Test with a toothpick. Remove from oven, turn pans on sides, and allow to set for a few minutes before taking from pans.
13. When cold, drip 2 tablespoons of wine over each cake.
14. As soon as it is absorbed, wrap tightly in cellophane freezer paper and store in refrigerator or freezer. They improve with age.
